methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Ark Robotics is a seed-stage company in the Defense & Security sector founded by Achi Takagama. The valuation universe comprises 10 comparable companies: 2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems drawn from a broader pool of 19 same-sector participants.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The narrow peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables—only 0 same-stage datapoints and 1 adjacent-stage peer emerged from screening, falling short of the 3-company minimum required for statistical reliability. The broader all-sector median approach would introduce material distortion given stage and mission-criticality differences within Defense & Security.

Without triangulated multiples-based evidence, this analysis cannot support a specific valuation range. Forward-looking analysis would require either (1) expansion of comparable set depth through secondary diligence on private peer financing rounds, (2) revenue or customer traction multiples if available, or (3) reliance on venture-stage benchmarks (e.g., seed-round typical post-money ranges in deep-tech). The principal uncertainty remains the lack of narrow-set precedent for seed-stage Defense & Security robotics valuations in the available data.
